expected_effect: Recover some calm-side edge without replaying the broad cheapening that broke the first state-layer pivot.
expected_failure_signature: If the veto suppresses fair-mid release too broadly, calm slices stay too expensive or the branch slides back into arb leakage.
expected_win_condition: Preserve the raw anchor quote assembly while reducing calm-path overpricing enough to convert the improving variant into a promoted one.
hidden_coupling_removed: Fair-mid recentering should not automatically inherit the same persistence memory that arms toxic-side quoting.
rationale: Separate toxic continuation memory from benign recentering by letting the fair-mid update veto only aligned toxic drift while preserving calm release.
title: Continuation veto fair mid
why_not_coefficient_retune: This adds a new veto contract between continuation state and fair-mid updates instead of changing the strength of an existing alpha.
